evals.expected_output: Parallel autonomous implementation of both stories, each in an isolated worktree with its own branch and PR, with post-batch quality review
expectations: The skill validates each story code exists in SmartSuite before launching
The skill reports the list of stories that will be implemented with their titles
The skill launches one Agent per story with isolation: worktree
All agents are launched in a single message (parallel, not sequential)
Each agent receives a prompt that invokes /omc-rails-autopilot with the story code
Each agent runs with run_in_background: true
The skill sets up a monitoring loop after launching agents
The skill reports a progress summary table showing story, status, branch, and PR columns
Each completed story results in a separate git branch (not all changes on one branch)
Each completed story results in a separate PR
The skill offers to run a post-batch quality pass (/dhh-review and /bali-view-audit) on completed PRs
SmartSuite story statuses are updated during execution

evals.expected_output: Fetches 3 stories from SmartSuite backlog and launches parallel agents in isolated worktrees
expectations: The skill queries SmartSuite for the top 3 backlog stories scoped to the current project
The skill reports which stories were found with their codes and titles before launching
The skill launches 3 parallel agents in isolated worktrees
Each agent runs the full omc-rails-autopilot workflow independently
The skill does not launch agents for stories that are not in backlog status

evals.prompt: Implement these 5 stories and let me review them tomorrow: GC-FND-002-US01 GC-FND-002-US02 GC-FND-003-US01 GC-FND-003-US02 GC-FND-004-US01
evals.expected_output: Launches 5 parallel agents with monitoring, designed for overnight autonomous execution
expectations: The skill launches all 5 agents in parallel (single message, not sequential)
Each agent gets its own isolated worktree
The skill warns about resource usage when launching 5 concurrent stories
The skill sets up a monitoring loop to track progress across all 5 stories
The batch continues running without requiring user interaction
Each story produces an independent PR that can be reviewed separately
